text: <par>J. H. Lorrain (1907:229) glosses this word as 'to bring up; to tend; to rear; to keep; to support; to feed (as child, etc.).'</par>

<par>Used only in the elaborate expression <latinform>ənúʔ-enāi</latinform>. There is perhaps a connection with Jg. <latinform>ʔnāi</latinform> 'sticky; adhesive; pliable; elastic' < PTB<reconstruction>*ʔ/s-n(w)ay </reconstruction>[see JAM-GSTC #97], since the brain is sometimes perceived as a sticky object. Cf. Lahu <latinform>ú-nɔ̀ʔ-nɛ̂ʔ</latinform>.</par>

<par>Used only in the elaborate expression <latinform>ənúʔ-enāi</latinform>. Perhaps related to Jingpho <latinform>ʔnāi</latinform> 'sticky; adhesive; pliable; elastic', since the brain is sometimes perceived as a sticky object.</par>
